notes:
Objective: plaintiff opening statement for arb-ex12-20260630154810. Proposition: by May 24, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, Iran initiated a major non-weather closure of its airspace within the market rule. Opportunity openings:plaintiff; allowed final act record_opening_statement only; no evidence submission permitted.

Record inspection: called wait_for_opportunity first; ready opportunity id openings:plaintiff. Used get_case/list_evidence/stat/read for all five packet items. Current record has five system case-packet files, no prior filings, no submitted_evidence. Evidence read: ev_6c99672d9a2c_market-page (market rule/API capture), ev_9f8061d601c0_primary-evidence (no merits primary packet; identifies categories), ev_72c545ccb705_official-source-record (official source packet absent; lists source examples), ev_4a5f41c923ac_secondary-reporting (secondary packet absent), ev_f994792e9085_unresolved-record-gaps (lists material gaps). Metadata checked: all case_packet/juror_visible/text/plain; hashes preserved in /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

What record proves: market rule text and operative elements. Yes requires Iran-initiated, non-weather, major closure by cutoff. Major closure includes broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian region, or non-weather complete arrivals/departures suspension affecting at least two listed airports (IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN). Primary resolution sources are official Iranian aviation authority information and consensus credible reporting. Packet also proves its own gaps: no official May 24 CAO/NOTAM/airport records, no secondary reporting packet, no exact timing proof, no proof yet separating Iran-initiated closure from airline/foreign action.

What record does not yet prove: an actual May 24 qualifying closure, exact ET cutoff coverage, official source chain, UMA proposal/dispute rationale, or whether the scope was sufficiently major rather than partial/regional/airline-specific/foreign/weather.

Native investigation/search leads (not record proof yet): web search queries for Iran May 24 2026 airspace closure/CAA/NOTAM. Leads found: Indian Express search result referencing NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 issued May 22 and valid through May 25, shutting all airports in western Tehran FIR; x.com/sentdefender snippet on CAA.IRI NOTAM May 22-May 25 and permission requirement; SafeAirspace Iran page on later partial reopening/western FIR remaining closed; WANA later report of western airspace closure. These are only source targets until captured/submitted in a later evidence phase.

Source map for later: official CAO/CAA Iran notice, NOTAM repository for OIIX A1010/26, airport authority notices for IKA/THR/MHD/SYZ/IFN, credible reporting following source links, adverse checks for western-only scope, exceptions, weather/military drill, airline/foreign restrictions, exact UTC/ET times.

Opening decision: file a plaintiff opening that frames the decisive question and burden, acknowledges current packet gaps, and says what evidence should decide the case. Avoid claiming unadmitted web leads as proof.

Opening opportunity openings:defendant. Record inspected via get_case/list_evidence/stat/read_evidence_range. Only five case-packet evidence files and plaintiff opening visible; no merits evidence submitted. Packet establishes rule and gaps: ev_6c99672d9a2c_market-page defines Yes as Iran-initiated major non-weather closure by May 24 2026 11:59 PM ET; major closure must be broad commercial flight closure/cancellation/suspension for Iranian airspace or major Iranian airspace region, or complete non-weather commercial arrivals/departures suspension affecting at least two listed airports (IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN). Limited cancellations/delays/partial closures, weather, airline/foreign-government restrictions do not suffice; limited exceptions to an otherwise broad closure do not disqualify. ev_9f8061d601c0_primary-evidence and ev_72c545ccb705_official-source-record say no merits packet or official-source packet was built and parties must discover/submit public evidence before relying on it. ev_f994792e9085_unresolved-record-gaps identifies missing UMA/dispute rationale, precipitating source, official Iranian aviation records/NOTAMs/airport ops records, contemporaneous reporting, causation, scope, and exact ET timing. Native web searches for orientation only: queries on Iran airspace closure May 24 2026 NOTAM, site:cao.ir, Iran Civil Aviation Organization May 24, Flightradar24 May 24. Leads found include snippets referencing NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 issued May 22 closing western Tehran FIR/all western airports through May 25; SafeAirspace June summary re partial reopening/western closure; social snippets from CNN-News18 etc. These are not admitted evidence and should not be cited as proof. Source plan for later: capture original NOTAM or official CAO/CAA record; verify UTC/ET validity; identify geographic/airport scope; check listed airport suspension; adverse checks for no-new-NOTAM/correction, airport operations continuing, permission regime, airline/foreign advisories, or partial/narrow region. Defendant opening theory: admitted record proves only rule and missing links. Plaintiff bears preponderance on issuer, timing, non-weather basis, and major scope. Defense should warn against resolving on snippets, flight maps, market dispute status, or assumed meaning of western-airspace references. A later official NOTAM may matter, but if it is only western/partial/permission-based or lacks two listed airport complete suspensions, it may not satisfy the rule.

Arguments:plaintiff work notes. Record rescan via get_case/list_evidence showed original five case-packet items plus plaintiff/defendant openings; no new evidence before my submissions. Defendant opening argues burden/precision and says western/partial/permission regime may fail.

Source plan: prove issuer, cutoff timing, scope/majority, non-weather cause. Searched exact identifiers and phrases: OIIX A1010/26, Tehran FIR western airports, Iran May 22 May 25 NOTAM. Opened Indian Express, Kenya Times, Abdi Media, Siasat, SafeAirspace leads. Could not capture an original official NOTAM from an official repository in available time; avoided relying on unsubmitted social snippets.

Submitted evidence accepted: (1) ev_0d618bc7936d_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-0d618bc7936d, Kenya Times May 23 report: NOTAM OIIX A1010/26; all airports in western Tehran FIR closed except limited daylight facilities; effective May 22 20:46Z to May 25 08:30Z; prior permissions suspended; fresh Iran CAA approvals required. (2) ev_3db793b61e1b_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-3db793b61e1b, Indian Express May 23 report: Iran closed western half May 22-May 25; Iranian Civil Aviation Authority issued NOTAM OIIX A1010/26; all western Tehran FIR airports shutdown; eight airports operational daylight only with per-flight CAA approval; rerouting observed. (3) ev_b01b937ea525_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-b01b937ea525, Abdi Media May 23 report: A1010/26; all western Tehran FIR airports closed except few named limited airports; previous permissions canceled; Civil Aviation Organization authorization required; valid to May 25 noon Iran time; security/military control context.

Time conversion: May 24 2026 11:59 PM ET = May 25 03:59Z (EDT). Kenya Times validity to May 25 08:30Z covers cutoff. Abdi May 25 noon Iran time (UTC+3:30) also equals 08:30Z. Abdi start time differs from Kenya Times, but end/cutoff coverage is consistent and start discrepancy is immaterial.

Merits reasoning: best path is broad closure of a major Iranian airspace region, not necessarily the two-listed-airport complete-suspension path, because the sources describe limited daylight exceptions at named airports. Rule allows limited exceptions to a broad closure. Iran initiation shown by reports of Civil Aviation Authority/Organization NOTAM and approval requirement. Non-weather shown by conflict/security context, not weather. Major shown by western half/western sector of Tehran FIR, all western airports closed except limited daylight operations, prior permits cancelled, CAA reapproval required, rerouting.

Weight/adverse audit: evidence is secondary/translated reporting and not the official NOTAM, so provenance is imperfect. However, three independent-ish contemporaneous captures agree on identifier A1010/26, authority, western Tehran FIR airport closure, exceptions, authorization requirement, and May 25 validity. Defense may argue partial/regional/permission regime; response: market text expressly includes major Iranian airspace region and limited exceptions do not disqualify broad closure.

Argument opportunity arguments:defendant. Record inspected via get_case/list_evidence/read_evidence_range. Plaintiff added three secondary text captures: ev_0d618bc7936d Kenya Times A1010/26 (western Tehran FIR airports closed except limited daylight facilities, May 22 20:46Z-May 25 08:30Z, prior permissions suspended, new CAA approvals; not original NOTAM); ev_3db793b61e1b Indian Express citing Gulf News/NOTAM (Iran CAA issued A1010/26, western Tehran FIR all airports, only eight airports operational including THR/IKA/IFN/SYZ/MHD etc with daylight/fresh approval; secondary); ev_b01b937ea525 Abdi Media translated summary (A1010/26, western Tehran FIR closures, exceptions at Tabriz/Kermanshah/Ahvaz/Shiraz/Yazd/Kerman/Kish/Abadan, validity stated May 22 00:16 Iran time to May 25 noon; secondary and timing differs from Kenya Times). Plaintiff technical report admits no original official NOTAM captured. I searched exact identifiers: OIIX A1010/26, A1010/26 Tehran FIR, Gulf News Iran western airspace May 23 2026, OIIX A1010/26 NOTAM. Found Notamify raw NOTAM page for OIIX A1015/26 and submitted as defense evidence ev_f736eb543e7c_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-f736eb543e7c. It states A1015/26 NOTAMR A1010/26, issued/effective May 23 18:21Z to May 25 08:30Z, all ACFT from/to/over FIR not permitted to enter Tehran FIR without permission, all airports in western Tehran FIR except OIII/OIIE/OIFM/OISS/OITL/OIYY/OITR/OICC/OIGG/OIAA/OIAM/OIAW closed, civil airlines to apply for new permission in western FIR for IFR passenger flights, previous permissions suspended, only IFR passenger flight in western FIR allowed. Defense theory: A1010 secondary summaries are stale at cutoff because A1015 replaced them; A1015 has more exceptions and a permission regime, defeating the listed-airport complete-suspension path and creating doubt over whether western Tehran FIR with many exceptions is a rule-defined major region closure. Bad fact/adverse: A1015 still strongly confirms an Iran NOTAM, non-weather security context, and broad western FIR restrictions through cutoff. If council treats western Tehran FIR as a major Iranian airspace region and exceptions as limited, plaintiff likely prevails. Argument should focus on burden, source-chain weakness, supersession, conflicting secondary reports, exception-heavy operative text, and lack of proof that western Tehran FIR is a major Iranian airspace region rather than a partial regional restriction.

Rebuttals:plaintiff work notes. Record scanned; defendant submitted ev_f736eb543e7c_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-f736eb543e7c, Notamify raw NOTAM OIIX A1015/26 replacing A1010/26. I stat/read the exact exhibit. Metadata: submitted_evidence, text/plain, Notamify source URL, retrieval 2026-06-30T16:01:30Z, sha256 f736eb543e7c927519c42f6c43f328b9646ec2d9d2783a4b3e9b9e05cbd9be0e.

Exact defendant NOTAM text: A1015/26 NOTAMR A1010/26; A) OICK; B) 2605231821; C) 2605250830; REF TEHRAN FIR; ALL ACFT FM/TO/OVER FIRS NOT PERMITTED TO ENTER TEHRAN FIR WITHOUT PERMISSION; ALL AIRPORTS IN THE WESTERN PART OF TEHRAN FIR EXC OIII, OIIE, OIFM, OISS, OITL, OIYY, OITR, OICC, OIGG, OIAA, OIAM, OIAW CLSD; civil airlines must apply for new permission in western Tehran FIR for IFR passenger flights; all previous permissions in western Tehran FIR suspended; only IFR passenger flight in western Tehran FIR allowed. Effective May 23 18:21Z to May 25 08:30Z.

Rebuttal theory: defendant exhibit does not defeat plaintiff; it confirms an operative replacement NOTAM in force at cutoff and continuing to May 25 08:30Z. It shows a broad permission/closure regime, including all aircraft entering Tehran FIR without permission barred, western airports closed except listed exceptions, previous permissions suspended, and only IFR PAX flights allowed in western FIR. Market rule permits major-region closures and limited exceptions; it does not require a complete nationwide shutdown. Listed-airport complete-suspension path is unnecessary. Notamify is an aggregator, but defendant offered it as raw NOTAM and it corroborates plaintiff source captures.

Surrebuttal opportunity surrebuttals:defendant. Record checked with get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff rebuttal exists and argues defendant's A1015 exhibit confirms the case: it was effective through cutoff, bars aircraft entering Tehran FIR without permission, closes western airports except listed exceptions, requires new permission for civil IFR passenger flights, and the market rule allows limited exceptions. No new source evidence beyond plaintiff rebuttal technical report/re-offered market rule and A1015; evidence list unchanged. Surrebuttal plan: answer narrow point only. Distinguish limited-exception clause: it saves an otherwise proven broad major-region closure, but does not prove that an undefined western part of Tehran FIR with many named exceptions and permission-based IFR passenger service is itself a major Iranian airspace region. Reemphasize source weight: official NOTAM still not captured; A1015 is Notamify aggregator text; plaintiff's A1010 reports are stale/conflicting and cannot supply the missing scope proof. Preserve listed-airport path failure: plaintiff's own sources and A1015 show key listed airports were not completely suspended. Bad fact: A1015 strongly supports Iran-issued non-weather restrictions through cutoff; council may find Yes if it treats western Tehran FIR as major and exceptions as limited. Defense focus remains burden and precision.

Closings:plaintiff work notes. Final record scanned with get_case/list_evidence/stat. No new evidence after defendant A1015/26 exhibit. Defendant surrebuttal concedes A1015/26 describes an Iran-issued, non-weather aviation restriction in force at the cutoff; argues only that it is not proved to be a market-defined major closure.

Closing posture: rely on record only. Key record includes market rule ev_6c99672d9a2c_market-page; plaintiff reporting exhibits ev_0d618bc7936d, ev_3db793b61e1b, ev_b01b937ea525; defendant raw NOTAM capture ev_f736eb543e7c. The strongest proof is A1015/26 effective until May 25 08:30Z (cutoff May 25 03:59Z), plus terms: all aircraft entering Tehran FIR require permission; all airports in western part closed except exceptions; civil airlines need new permission; prior permissions suspended; only IFR passenger flights allowed in western FIR. Strongest defense point: Notamify is aggregator and no official Iranian artifact, region-major inference, many exceptions. Response: preponderance, defendant supplied raw NOTAM capture, rule expressly permits limited exceptions and major-region closures. This is more than limited cancellation/delay; even defense concedes Iran-issued non-weather restriction at cutoff.

Closing opportunity closings:defendant. Record scanned via get_case/list_evidence. No new evidence after surrebuttal; plaintiff closing filed and argues the only remaining issue is major-closure element. Plaintiff emphasizes timing (A1015 and Kenya Times end May 25 08:30Z after cutoff), Iran initiation/non-weather as conceded, and uses defendant's A1015 exhibit to show broad Tehran FIR permission/closure regime with limited exceptions allowed by rule. Closing strategy: concede serious Iran-issued non-weather restriction in force through cutoff; focus on burden and definition of major closure. Plaintiff still relies on inference that western part of Tehran FIR equals a major Iranian airspace region; record lacks official Iranian artifact, geography/map, traffic volume, and operational proof. Listed-airport complete-suspension path fails because plaintiff and A1015 show listed airports operational/excepted under daylight/approval restrictions. Emphasize limited-exceptions clause saves an otherwise established broad major closure; it cannot prove the threshold when the operative text is an exception-heavy permission regime. Acknowledge adverse point: council may find Yes if it treats western Tehran FIR as major and permission-based restrictions as limited exceptions. No new evidence or technical reports can be submitted in closing.
